data.narrative:
Allen Control Systems is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Mike Wior, Steve Simoni, and Luke Allen. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—across the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $42M in total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a $84M valuation. The peer-median method examines four same-stage, same-sector comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M). The median across this set is $225M, with a range from $125M to $500M.

Triangulating these two approaches yields a valuation range of $71M (low) to $259M (high), with a median point of $154M. The wide dispersion between methods reflects material uncertainty around Allen's competitive positioning relative to better-capitalized peers and the stage-appropriate risk premium. Key variables driving valuation within this band include customer concentration, technology differentiation within defense applications, and near-term growth trajectory relative to comparables at similar funding stages.
